name: HCW Biologics Inc.
business: HCW Biologics Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing fusion immunotherapeutics for diseases promoted by chronic inflammation, including autoimmune disorders, cancer, and senescence-associated dysplasia. It also markets commercial-ready proprietary compounds for use as reagents in the production of immunotherapeutics for infectious diseases and cancer. The company generates revenue through licensing agreements and supply of clinical materials to partners such as Wugen and Trimmune.

exposures.sentence: HCW Biologics Inc. (HCW Biologics or the Company) is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing transformative fusion immunotherapeutics to support or treat diseases promoted by chronic inflammation.

catalysts.event: First patient dosed in Phase 1 clinical trial for HCW9302 in alopecia areata
catalysts.sentence: On November 17, 2025, the first patient was dosed at The Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center for the Company-sponsored, multi-center first-in-human clinical trial to evaluate HCW9302 in patients with alopecia areata (NCT07049328).

catalysts.event: Preliminary human data read out for HCW9302 Phase 1 trial
catalysts.sentence: A preliminary human data read out for this study is expected in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Completion of IND-enabling studies for HCW11-018b
catalysts.sentence: IND-enabling studies are expected to be completed in first half of 2027.
We intend to complete IND-enabling studies and submit an IND application to evaluate HCW11-018b, our tetra-valent T-cell engager, in patients with solid tumors in the first half of 2027.

catalysts.event: Completion of IND-enabling studies for HCW11-040
catalysts.sentence: IND-enabling studies are expected to be completed in second half of 2027.
We intend to complete IND-enabling studies and submit an IND application to evaluate HCW11-040, our second-generation immune checkpoint inhibitor, in patients with BPD in the second half of 2027.
As of March 16, 2026, we received the full payment of the upfront licensing fee for the exclusive worldwide license for HCW11-006, a preclinical molecule, from Trimmune.

catalysts.event: Phase 1 clinical trial initiation for HCW11-006 in China by Trimmune
catalysts.sentence: Trimmune intends to begin Phase 1 clinical trials in China in mid-2027.

catalysts.event: Expiration of suspension period for Wugen License
catalysts.sentence: The suspension will run for a period of one year from the effective date of the suspension and will end on May 29, 2026.
On February 19, 2026, the Company raised $1.5 million before commission and transaction costs payable by us through the sale of 2,477,292 Units for $0.6055 per Unit.

tamClaims.claim: Alopecia areata affects approximately 1 in 1,000 people, with a lifetime incidence of 2% worldwide, or 160 million people. About 7 million people in the United States have alopecia areata.
tamClaims.sentence: Alopecia is one of the most prevalent autoimmune diseases in the world, affecting approximately 1 in 1,000 people, with a lifetime incidence of 2% worldwide, or 160 million people.

tamClaims.claim: Pancreatic cancer: an estimated 66,440 Americans diagnosed and more than 51,750 died in 2024; worldwide approximately 550,000 new cases and 500,000 deaths in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: In the U.S. alone, an estimated 66,440 Americans were diagnosed with pancreatic cancer and more than 51,750 died from the disease in 2024.

tamClaims.claim: Ovarian cancer: fifth leading cause of cancer-related death among women in the U.S.; five-year survival about 49%-54%.
tamClaims.sentence: In the United States, while ovarian cancer is the 11th most common cancer among women, it is the fifth leading cause of cancer-related death among women.

tamClaims.claim: Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia (BPD) affects 10,000-15,000 babies annually in the U.S.; market size expected to reach USD 472.0 million by 2035.
tamClaims.sentence: The number of newborns suffering with Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia (BPD) is growing due to rising premature birth rates and improved survival rates of extremely preterm infants, particularly in infants born at 23-27 weeks gestation, affecting 10,000-15,000 babies annually in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: Global botulinum toxin market valued at $6.4 billion in 2022, growing at CAGR of 11.50% from 2022 to 2030, expected to reach $15.2 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: For example, the global botulinum toxin market was valued at $6.4 billion (USD) in 2022 and growing at a CAGR of 11.50% from 2022 to 2030. The market is expected to reach $15.2 billion (USD) by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Alzheimer's disease: health care and long-term care costs projected to reach $384 billion in 2025 and nearly $1 trillion in 2050 in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the Alzheimer's Association, for the United States, health care and long-term care costs for people living with dementia were projected to reach $384 billion in 2025 and nearly $1 trillion in 2050.

tamClaims.claim: Organ transplants: record-breaking 46,632 organ transplants performed in the United States in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the United Network for Organ Sharing, in 2025, there were a record-breaking 46,632 organ transplants performed in the United States.
